result:
pool keeps set of open db connections alive
reuse across requests instead of opening new socket each time
opening connection costly: tcp handshake, tls, auth, session setup — tens of ms
pool amortizes that cost over many queries

client asks pool for connection, runs query, returns it
if all busy, caller waits or gets error
idle connections may be closed after timeout

key knobs:
min size — warm connections kept ready
max size — ceiling to avoid overwhelming db
acquire timeout — how long to wait for free conn
idle timeout — when to reap unused
max lifetime — force recycle to dodge stale state

sizing: too small → queue builds, latency spikes
too big → db runs out of workers/memory, contention worsens
rule of thumb: max_conns ≈ cpu_cores * 2 + spindles, but depends on workload

gotchas:
transaction state leaks if conn returned mid-tx
prepared statements tied to connection in some drivers
long-running query hogs slot
pgbouncer etc. add external pool layer in front of postgres for many app instances
